reasoning: The massive January 2025 'AI Diffusion Rule', which introduced broad global controls on AI model weights (ECCN 4E091) and advanced chips, was officially rescinded by the BIS on May 13, 2025, just before its compliance date. The stated reasons included being 'overly bureaucratic' and risking 'American innovation'. On the same day, BIS issued alternative targeted measures (like end-use controls and anti-diversion red flags) and has since focused primarily on China-specific restrictions (e.g., April 2025 updates on chips to China). Because the resolution criteria require controls that 'apply broadly, and must not be targeted or limited to a single country or entity', these China-focused rules do not qualify. While BIS is reportedly working on replacement rules for the rescinded global AI framework, as of late October 2025 there has been no official broad replacement published. Given the current administration's stance against sweeping global AI controls, and the short window remaining before December 31, 2025, the likelihood of a qualifying broadly-applicable new rule taking effect before year-end is low. I estimate the probability at 25%, reflecting the remaining chance of a replacement rule by December.
